sections.paragraph:
παρέρχομαι [παρά, ἔρχομαι]

—1. ‘move spatially from one position to another’

—a. go past Mk 6:48; Ac 27:9.

—b. pass by, go along Mt 8:28; pass by Lk 18:37.

—c. go through, of passage without social inolvement Ac 16:8; 17:15 v.l.

—d. come (alongside) Lk 12:37; 17:7; Ac 24:7 v.l.

—e. in extended sense of ignoring what ought to be done pass by Lk 11:42; transgress 15:29.

—2. ‘come to an end and so no longer be on the scene’, pass (away), of things Mt 5:18a; 24:35a; 26:39, 42; Mk 14:35; 2 Cor 5:17; 2 Pt 3:10; of humans Mt 24:34; Lk 21:32; Js 1:10. Sim. in the sense lose force Mt 5:18b; 24:35b; Lk 21:33b. Of time pass Mt 14:15; Ac 27:9; 1 Pt 4:3.
